column1: lorem-svg-data-uri-css-background,"A small inline SVG used as a CSS background-image via url(data:image/svg+xml
column2: utf8,...). Common pattern for icons rendered through stylesheet rules rather than tags. Angle brackets in the SVG are URL-encoded (%3C, %3E) — this is the canonical form for SVG-in-CSS data URIs because the HTML parser treats but DOM parsers and serializers can mis-interpret literal `` inside CSS, so url-encoding is the standard practice. The SVG must survive postcss-based CSS sanitization without the base64 gate that applies to raster image MIME types.",lorem,lorem,none,issue #143 — svg+xml in CSS url() image-context loading,"
